info.title: gc
info.description: dotnet-git-conventional can generate changelog files from the Git history by utilizing conventional commits and semantic versioning in combination with a wide flexibility by providing a broad set of configuration possiblities.

commands.name: changelog
commands.description: Generates a changelog based on the commits.

options.name: --config
options.description: Additional configuration files to load. They will be merged in order of occurence.

options.name: --output
options.description: Where to put the output file. If none is provided the output is written to the console.

options.name: --template
options.description: The template file to use for generating the changelog.

options.name: --dump
options.description: Dumps the acquired information to a json file.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.

commands.name: init
commands.description: Saves the default configuration file to the current repository root.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.

commands.name: schema
commands.description: Generates the configuration json schema file to import into your editor.

options.name: --output
options.description: Where to put the output file. [default: conventional.configuration.schema.json]

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Validates the commit message in the given file. Please put the command 'gc validate --msg-file $1' into the commit-msg hook.

options.name: --msg-file
options.description: The path to file containing the git message.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.
